short_term.title: Short-term view (0–30 days · next earnings cycle)
bullets: Run-rate $1134.10B in FY2026 Q1, +35.1% YoY — accelerating trajectory entering the next print.
Next-quarter consensus: $25.40B (-97.8% vs current Q) — set by 30 broker contributors.
EPS consensus next qtr: $2.95.
Margin context: 50.5% net margin (58.1% op margin) — expansion likely.
Next earnings: 2026-07-16 — primary catalyst for the short-term window.
short_term.takeaway: Going into the next earnings print, the setup is constructive — consensus is achievable based on current run-rate.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ days · multi-year setup)
bullets: TTM revenue $4104B — in cyclical drawdown.
Sell-side: FY1 $94B → FY2 $108B (+14.9% multi-year top-line implied).
Forward EBIT margin: 28.0% — structural premium business over the long-cycle view.
6-quarter run-rate CAGR: +23.8% — the secular trajectory the market is pricing.
Sector thesis (Information Technology): Multi-year AI/cloud capex cycle remains the dominant secular theme.
long_term.takeaway: Long-cycle thesis is intact if growth durability + margin expansion both hold.

short_term.title: Short-term view (0–30 days · trading window)
bullets: Price $401.62 vs SMA50 $370.70 (+8.3%) — short-term trend is bullish (price holding above the near-term moving average).
RSI(14): 52.4 — neutral / no edge.
MACD: 7.92 vs signal 10.52 — bearish crossover.

Bollinger band position: 51% of band width (band width 9.2% — normal).

short_term.takeaway: Short-term trader bias: long (trend + momentum aligned bullish).

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ days · positioning window)
bullets: Price $401.62 vs SMA200 $316.10 (+27.1%) — long-term trend is in **uptrend intact**.
Moving average regime: Golden cross active (SMA50 > SMA200) — institutional trend filter bullish.

long_term.takeaway: Long-term positioning: accumulate on weakness — structural trend supportive.

short_term.title: Short-term view (0–45 DTE · front-month tactical)
bullets: Front-month ATM IV: 56.6% — elevated (premium-selling favored).
Put/Call OI: 2.03 — put-heavy hedging (institutions defensive).
30-day implied move: ±11.2% ($356.54 – $446.70) — the 1-σ range the options market is pricing.
ATM skew: -1.56pp — call skew (upside bid — bullish positioning).
Front-month OI cluster: $340.0 — likely magnet / pin-risk for expiry.
short_term.strategy: Bull call spread 30 DTE: long $402 / short $422. Mid IV (57%) + uptrend → defined-risk directional with reasonable cost basis.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ DTE · LEAPS / structural)
bullets: Back-month avg IV (90+ DTE): 46.6% across 10 expiries.
Available long-dated expiries: 10 dates spanning 93–940 DTE.
90-day implied move: ±23.0% ($309.41 – $493.83).
393-day implied move: ±48.7% ($205.90 – $597.34) — the long-dated 1-σ range.
Sell-side PT vs options-implied range: consensus $250 (-37.8% upside). Compare to the 90+ DTE implied range above.
Term structure in backwardation (-10.0pp front→back) — near-term event premium; long-dated vol cheaper.
long_term.strategy: Sell 6-month strangle: short $361 put / short $442 call. Range-bound + high IV → premium-selling on both sides; profits if spot stays within ±10%; manage with rolls or assignment.
